correctness.justification: All spec requirements implemented: Path normalization, parent directory creation, temp file in same directory, os.replace for atomic rename, cleanup in except block, text/binary mode detection based on content type, permission preservation, TypeError for invalid content. All 17 acceptance tests pass. Uses BaseException in except to catch KeyboardInterrupt too, which is good.

edge_cases.justification: Handles: empty string/bytes content, non-existent parent directories, overwriting existing files, permission preservation, TypeError for int/list content, string paths, custom encoding. The cleanup uses try/except OSError around os.unlink which is proper best-effort cleanup.

code_quality.justification: Clean 75-line implementation. Full type hints including union types (str | Path, str | bytes, int | None). Good use of pathlib.Path. The mode parameter is accepted but effectively ignored (actual mode determined by content type) - the docstring notes this honestly. Uses NamedTemporaryFile correctly with delete=False and context manager for writing.

documentation.justification: Module docstring explains the atomic write strategy. Function docstring covers Args (all 4 parameters), Raises (TypeError, OSError), and describes the permission preservation behavior. The note about mode being informational-only is transparent. No inline comments needed given the clear code structure.

test_coverage.justification: 13 tests covering: text write, bytes write, overwrite, nested directory creation, empty string, empty bytes, TypeError for int and list, string path, permission preservation, default permissions for new file, custom encoding, no temp file left on success. Missing: test that temp file is cleaned up on write failure, test for concurrent writes, test with symlinks, test that os.replace (not os.rename) is used.

robustness.justification: Strong error handling: TypeError raised early with descriptive message, BaseException catch ensures cleanup even on KeyboardInterrupt, best-effort temp file cleanup with suppressed OSError, permission preservation with proper stat/chmod flow. The except block correctly uses 'raise' to re-raise the original exception after cleanup.
